strategy: Buy $265 Call — Sep 2026 (deep dip buy, 40% upside)

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $263.45 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$26,300 cash. Exceeds $10,000 per-contract threshold for a standard retail portfolio. Use Calls/Spreads strategy only.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: N/A — Wheel not applicable for this stock

exitRules.entryTrigger: Stock at $294.46 — call is $29.46 in-the-money (+12% since analysis). Enter on any pullback toward $278–285 for better risk/reward. Reassess position sizing given the move.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 50–70% gain on premium. First target: stock at $330 (+12% from current) for ~$65 call value. Second target: $368+ analyst consensus for full upside.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not maintained above $285 by ~July 10 (60% of time elapsed on Nov position), reduce or close. Do not hold a degrading ITM call past support breakdown.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 35–40% loss on premium. Deep ITM calls carry significant dollar risk on stock drops.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by August 21 (5 days before August 26 Q3 earnings). IV crush at 67% is severe — no exceptions.

options.bestFor: Not applicable — Aug 15 expiry (Day 96) is 6 days BEFORE the Aug 21 mandatory earnings close deadline. Option expires before you can cleanly exit ahead of earnings.
Marginal — only 29 days left at the Aug 21 close. Most time value will have decayed leaving essentially intrinsic only. Avoid unless entering very close to earnings.

Recommended — 92 days of time value at the Aug 21 exit. Deep ITM call with meaningful time premium. Gives MDB the full Q2 and pre-Q3 catalyst window. High cost reflects the stock's +12% move since analysis.

recommendation.title: Single-Leg Call — ATM Dip Buy
recommendation.entryNote: Stock down 47% from $444.72 52-week high — significant dip buy opportunity. Q1 FY2026 already reported: revenue $549M (+22% YoY, beat), Atlas 26% growth. No immediate earnings catalyst. IV ~50%. Vector database and agentic AI positioning gives long-term optionality. 33 analysts with Buy consensus at $368.67 avg target (+40% upside).

companyOverview: Leading NoSQL database platform with Atlas cloud service growing 26% YoY (72% of total revenue). Q1 FY2026: revenue $549M (+22%), EPS -$0.46 (beat -$0.85 est), FCF $105.9M. Expanding into agentic AI through Voyage AI integration and vector database capabilities. 8,000+ enterprises in MAAP program. Stock down 47% from peak despite fundamentals holding.
catalysts: Stock down 47% from peak — asymmetric dip buy with 40% upside to consensus
Atlas cloud platform growing 26% YoY — recurring revenue engine
Vector database integration with Voyage AI for agentic AI workloads
FCF positive and growing ($105.9M vs $61.0M prior year)
74% of enterprises planning integrated vector databases — MDB well-positioned
33 analysts with Buy consensus at $368 average target
risks: Growth decelerating from 30%+ to low-20s — narrative shift from 'hyper-growth' to 'growth'
Competition from Pinecone, Weaviate, and native vector database capabilities in other platforms
High R&D spend ($600M annually) vs. operating cash flow — not yet GAAP profitable
Stock down 47% already signals institutional concern about growth trajectory
Potential activist investor interest cited due to underperformance vs. peers
Enterprise budget caution if tech spending slows
dilution: Moderate SBC dilution typical for growth-stage software. No concerning capital raises. FCF positive and improving reduces need for equity financing.
